content:
Maths Buddy: Your AI Maths Tutor for Grade 8–12

Are you looking for a maths buddy — someone (or something) that can help you with maths anytime, anywhere? StudyBuddy's AI maths tutor is the closest thing to having a brilliant maths buddy available 24/7, for free.

Whether you're struggling with Grade 8 algebra, Grade 11 trigonometry, or Grade 12 calculus, our AI tutor gives you step-by-step explanations in plain language — just like a patient friend who's great at maths.

What Can a Maths Buddy Help You With?

Grade 8–9 Maths Topics

  • Integers, fractions, decimals, and percentages
  • Algebraic expressions and equations
  • Geometry (angles, triangles, parallel lines)
  • Data handling and probability
  • Measurement (perimeter, area, volume)

Grade 10 Maths Topics

  • Functions (linear, quadratic, hyperbola, exponential)
  • Algebraic equations and inequalities
  • Geometry and trigonometry basics
  • Number patterns and sequences
  • Financial mathematics

Grade 11 Maths Topics

  • Quadratic equations and inequalities
  • Trigonometry (identities, equations, 2D problems)
  • Analytical geometry
  • Euclidean geometry (proofs)
  • Probability and statistics

Grade 12 Maths Topics (NSC)

  • Sequences and series (arithmetic and geometric)
  • Functions (inverses, logarithms)
  • Calculus (differentiation, integration)
  • Trigonometry (3D problems, compound angles)
  • Analytical geometry (circles and lines)
  • Statistics (regression, probability)
  • Financial mathematics (annuities, future value)

Why StudyBuddy is the Best Maths Buddy

How to Use StudyBuddy as Your Maths Buddy

  1. Sign up for free at /students — no credit card needed
  2. Tell StudyBuddy your grade and the topic you need help with
  3. Ask any maths question — paste a problem, describe what you're stuck on, or ask for a full explanation of a topic
  4. Get step-by-step help — StudyBuddy explains each step and checks your understanding
  5. Practice with past papers — use the past paper library to test yourself

Student Results with StudyBuddy

  • Average grade improvement: 15–25% within 4–6 weeks
  • Most students go from failing to passing within a semester
  • Available any time — even at 11pm the night before your maths exam

"I was failing maths in Grade 11 and couldn't afford a tutor. StudyBuddy explained things step-by-step and I went from 38% to 64% in one term." — Sipho, Gauteng

Other Maths Buddy Resources

  • DBE Past Papers: Free NSC maths past papers at www.education.gov.za
  • Mindset Learn (YouTube): Free CAPS maths video lessons
  • Khan Academy: Free maths lessons (note: not CAPS-specific, but useful for foundations)
  • Siyavula: Free CAPS Maths and Science textbooks and practice

Ready to find your maths buddy? Start with StudyBuddy for free — no credit card required. Available 24/7 for all Grade 8–12 CAPS maths topics.
quickAnswer: StudyBuddy is South Africa's AI maths tutor for Grade 8–12 — your ultimate maths buddy. Available 24/7, covering all CAPS maths topics from Grade 8 to Grade 12 (NSC). Start free at /students — no credit card required.

faqs.question: What is a maths buddy?
faqs.answer: A maths buddy is a study partner or tool that helps you understand maths — explaining concepts, checking your work, and guiding you through difficult problems. StudyBuddy's AI tutor acts as a 24/7 maths buddy for South African students in Grade 8–12, covering all CAPS maths topics for free.

faqs.question: Where can I get free maths help in Grade 12?
faqs.answer: Free Grade 12 maths help is available from: (1) StudyBuddy AI tutor (free to start, CAPS-aligned, 24/7), (2) DBE past papers at education.gov.za, (3) Mindset Learn on YouTube, and (4) Siyavula (free CAPS textbooks and practice).

faqs.question: How can I improve my Grade 12 maths marks?
faqs.answer: To improve Grade 12 maths: (1) Practice with NSC past papers regularly, (2) Get personalised help from StudyBuddy AI when you're stuck, (3) Focus on understanding concepts (not memorising), (4) Identify your weak topics and work on them specifically. Most students improve 15–25% with consistent daily practice.
